sections.heading: Step 3: Authorization Code
sections.text:
Provide the login identifier and confirm the push notification on the mobile. SP should use below code to pass to Access token Call.

Note: Copy the {Authorization Code} to be used in next step to get the access token

Response:

sections.heading: Authentication Request
sections.text:
`GET` `https://stg-id.uaepass.ae/idshub/authorize`

Once the authentication of the user is complete, and the user has granted authorization, the application receives an HTTP GET request of the following type from the user’s browser. This HTTP request is an OAuth 2.0 authorization response. The application receives this request at the redirect URL specified in the authorization or authentication request message (the redirect_uri parameter) or in the registered redirect URL.

sections.heading: Query Parameters
tables: Must take the value, which indicates that an code authorization code is requested.
Redirect URI to the application. The application waits to receive at this URI the authorization or authentication response message with the authorization code.
Identifier of the client application. (To be shared by UAEPASS Team)

We recommend using this parameter to safeguard against CSRF attacks. The application can also include additional information in this parameter, such as the URL to which the browser is to be redirected when the authorization or authentication finishes. (To include multiple data in the value of this parameter, the application must serialize it as it sees fit.)

List of values, separated by spaces, that represent the scope of the authorization that the application wants to obtain. It queries the scopes required for accessing the resources or services in question. (To be shared by UAEPASS Team if its value is other than specified in sample above)
Defines conditions for authenticating the user (minimum levels or specific flows) who must authorize the access. (To be used as specified in sample or check with UAEPASS team for more details)
Language parameter to be sent to render English or Arabic login pages of UAEPASS and below are the possible values: English page : en Arabic page : ar

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: As per the diagram shown in figure 2, SP should obtain the access token by passing the same authorization code which was received in the previous step.

sections.heading: cURL Request for Token Generation Call
sections.text: Replace code parameter '{ code}' with the value received from Authorization Code.

sections.heading: API detail to exchange the token
sections.text: `POST` `https://stg-id.uaepass.ae/idshub/token`

sections.heading: Query Parameters
tables: Redirect URI to the application. The application waits to receive at this URI the authorization or authentication response message with the authorization code.
Authorization code received in the previous authorization response.

sections.heading: Headers
sections.text:
JSON object containing the access token, associated information and (if the scope was openid requested) an ID token.

access_token: Access token generated by UAE PASS.
token_type: Type of access token. Always has the “Bearer” value.
expires_in: Lifetime (in seconds) of the access token.
scope: Scopes granted to those to which the access token is associated, separated by spaces." %}

unsupported_grant_type: The value of the grant_type parameter in the request is not supported. TrustedX currently only supports the authorization_code type.
invalid_grant, codeNotFound: The authorization code specified in the code parameter of the request was not issued in a recent authorization response or has expired.
invalid_grant, expiredCode: The authorization code specified in the code parameter of the request has expired. This error appears when passing the expired code to token generation call. The auth code received from authorization url should be utilized by SP within 10 seconds.
invalid_grant, codeNotIssuedToClientId: The authorization code specified was issued for a different client application than that identified in the Authorization HTTP header.
invalid_grant, redirectUriMismatch: The authorization code specified was issued for an authorization request associated to a redirect URL different from the URL specified in the redirect_uri parameter of the request. Please make sure that SP passes the same redirect url in authentication and token generation calls.
invalid_grant, invalidOrExpiredCode: The authorization code specified in the code parameter of the request has expired or invalid.
invalid_scope: Only for the client credentials grant flow. One of the scopes requested by the application is not included in this list of scopes provided by the authorization server or has not been enabled for the client credentials grant. This error also occurs if the application did not request any scope and the server has no default scope defined for this type of grant.
Invalid_basic_header_authentication: This error appears when SP is not passing the client id or secret properly as configured in server.
Invalid_Token: Please make sure that SP is passing the token to user profile API using header Authorization as Bearer {token}. " %}

invalid_client, invalidCredentials: The client application cannot be authenticated with the credentials included in the Authorization HTTP header. This may be because there is no Client Application configured in TrustedX with the identifier specified or because the entity exists but the secret does not match the configured secret. " %}

sections.heading: API Details

sections.heading: Headers
tables: Bearer {access token received in previous step}

sections.heading: Here are the articles in this Section:
intro
sections.text: The UAE PASS Account Linking Guide outlines the required standards and recommended flows for securely associating a user’s UAE PASS identity with their local profile on a Service Provider’s platform. The goal is to ensure a seamless, secure, and user-friendly experience whether the linking is performed automatically or manually.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: User accounts should be automatically linked to UAE PASS using one or more of the following identifiers: UAE PASS Unique User Identifier (SPUUID), Emirates ID, or Unique ID. This approach ensures a seamless, secure, and user-transparent experience without requiring manual intervention.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: Manual linking is required when automatic matching is not possible or when multiple user records need to be clarified. The guide defines four key cases to cover different scenarios:

sections.heading: Two records found with the same "Unique Identifier"
sections.text:
If the Service Provider Component (SPC) detects two records with the same unique identifier—such as an Emirates ID number or email address—the system should prompt the user to select the correct account.

Example Scenarios:

- Record 1: 784-1234-1134567-1
- Record 2: 784-1234-1134567-1

Or

- Record 1:
- Record 2:

In such cases, SPC must display a selection screen allowing the user to choose which record to proceed with, ensuring accuracy and user control during the account linking process.

sections.heading: Matching User Record Identified
sections.text:
When the Service Provider detects two records with matching user information—one from UAE PASS and one from the local system—the system should automatically compare the data.

If a match is confirmed, the user will be prompted to log in using their existing Service Provider credentials (e.g., username and password) to securely link the accounts. This method ensures a smooth and secure account linking experience, enabling seamless access to services.

> The appropriate design screen for Case B should be displayed to guide the user through this step.

sections.heading: No record found with the same "Unique Identifier"
sections.text:
SPC is allowing online user registration,
Manual account linking shows one page with
both options:
If user has already local login, the user enters
local Service Provider username/
password and link accounts.

If the user doesn’t a have local login and SP is
allowing online user registration, then user
can start the sign-up process.

sections.heading: 1. Account Verification Prompt (No Unique Identifier Found)
sections.text:
If no unique key attribute is found (such as SPUUID, Emirates ID, email, or mobile number), the system should prompt the user with a question:
“Do you already have an account with the Service Provider?”

Depending on the user's response, apply the following flow:

- If Yes: Prompt the user to enter their local Service Provider credentials (username and password) to manually link the accounts.
- Apply the corresponding design guidelines to support this step and ensure a secure linking process.

sections.heading: 2. New User Creation
sections.text: If No: The Service Provider should initiate new user registration by prefilling the registration form using the verified data retrieved from UAE PASS. This ensures a streamlined onboarding process while minimizing manual data entry for the user.

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.text:
Follow these guidelines to offer a seamless experience with UAE PASS.

Use the recommended styles on websites and mobile apps.
Do not create your own button styles – maintain UAE PASS consistency.

Pick a button style that suits your platform’s design.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: This file contains all official UAE PASS button assets for use by service providers integrating with UAE PASS across web and mobile platforms.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The system provides several options of the buttons. Depending on the platform on which your
content runs, choose the variant that fits the terminology of your experience and use it consistently
throughout your interfaces.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Depending on the platform, the system provides up to three options for the appearance of the UAE
PASS buttons: white, white with an outline, and black. Choose the appearance that works best with
the background on which the button displays.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Adjust the corner radius to match your UI: choose from

1. Rectangle (Minimum radius)
2. Default (Standard radius, 12 pixels)
3. Pill-shaped (Maximum radius, 999)

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Minimum size:

- Width: 140pt
- Height: 30pt
- Margin: 1/10th of button height
Preserve aspect ratio.

Ensure the button scales correctly across devices and screen sizes, maintaining the aspect ratio so the UAE PASS logo remains unstretched.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Active and Disabled State. The active state is when a button is ‘clickable’/’tappable’. While that may
sound a bit obvious, the important thing to note here is that a button can be disabled if the user
hasn’t completed the necessary steps.

Focus and Pressed State. The focus state is when the button has been tabbed to. The pressed state
is the state when the user’s cursor or finger is ‘holding down’ on the button.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The system provides Arabic button titles. The Arabic buttons are available in the same styles and
different states as the English ones.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Continue with UAE PASS
Users should be clearly informed that by proceeding, they are sharing a digital copy of their documents for identity verification purposes. This step serves as a replacement for the traditional physical document verification process.

The “Continue with UAE PASS” capability may follow either the “Sign in” or “Sign up with UAE PASS” flow, but it must be clearly communicated to the user that this action involves sharing their personal documents.

Service providers are encouraged to highlight the advantages of using UAE PASS for document sharing and to provide users with clear guidance on how to upgrade their account and upload the necessary documents.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Case A: New Customer Onboarding
A new customer selects “Continue with UAE PASS” to share their documents as part of the onboarding process for accessing the service provider’s platform.

Case B: Accessing a Product or Service
A new or existing customer selects “Continue with UAE PASS” to share documents required to access a specific product or service offered by the provider.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Case C: KYC Document Update
An existing customer selects “Continue with UAE PASS” to update their KYC documents in response to a request from the service provider.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Preserve the capitalization style of the title. All variants of the button title capitalize the first word —
that is, Sign or Continue; all other letters are lowercase. Don't change this style by, for example,
capitalizing every letter in the title.

Keep the title and logo vertically aligned within the button. To do this, vertically align the title to the

middle of the button, then add the logo image, making sure its height matches the height of the button

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The black button style is available across all platforms and the web. It is best used on white or light-colored backgrounds that offer sufficient contrast.

Avoid using this style on black or dark backgrounds, as it may reduce visibility and compromise accessibility.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Within a button, the title text must be in either black or white—custom colors are not permitted.

For the UAE PASS logo, only two color variations are allowed:

- Black logo with green and red accents
- White logo with green and red accents

Avoid using fully black or fully white logos, and do not apply any custom color treatments to the logo under any circumstances.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The white outlined button style is supported across all platforms, including the web. It should be used on white or light-colored backgrounds where the standard, white-filled button does not provide sufficient contrast.

Avoid using this style on dark or saturated backgrounds, as the black outline may create unnecessary visual clutter. In such cases, use the solid white button style to ensure clear contrast and maintain visual clarity.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Do not display “Sign in” and “Sign up” buttons on the same page.
Each button should appear within its respective flow:

- Use the “Sign in” button exclusively on the sign-in page
- Use the “Sign up” button on the registration or create account page

This ensures a clear and user-friendly experience.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The service provider retains the flexibility to add any error icon to the system. However, we
recommend using specific icons that align with our notification function. While customization or
removal may be challenging; these recommended icons ensure clarity and effective communication.

images.caption: UAE PASS As a Title or normal text

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
- Avoid poor contrast. Ensure all user details and button text are clearly visible against the background.
- Maintain consistent alignment. Use the correct size and alignment to guide the user toward the intended action.
- Do not combine multiple actions in a single button. This can lead to confusion. Instead, separate actions clearly—each button should represent one distinct function, as demonstrated in the example.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
- Do not enlarge the UAE PASS logo beyond the recommended size. An oversized logo may interfere with surrounding content, as illustrated in the example.
- Ensure that both the text and button remain clear and legible. Use appropriate sizing and alignment to maintain a clean, user-friendly interface.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
The white style is available on all platforms and the web. Use this style on dark or colored
backgrounds that provide sufficient contrast.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
If using “Login” instead of “Sign in”, apply the same button guidelines.

Don’t mix both terms on one page.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: When using logo-only UAE PASS buttons, always maintain a 1:1 aspect ratio and avoid adding extra or horizontal padding, as the artwork already includes the necessary spacing. To fit the button into your UI design, use masks (such as circular or rounded square shapes) rather than cropping the logo or modifying its built-in padding. The logo must remain intact and unaltered to preserve branding integrity.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
If you intend to use the UAE PASS logo alongside additional text, such as “Powered by UAE PASS”, within your website or mobile application, you must obtain prior written consent or have an existing agreement with the UAE PASS team.

You may use the UAE PASS logo in a referential manner to indicate that your product is compatible with UAE PASS services, provided that you comply with the following requirements:

Any indication that your product or platform is supported by the UAE PASS service must be presented in a referential format. The recommended phrasing is:
“[Your Product Name], powered by UAE PASS”

Please refer to the example below for proper usage.

sections.heading: Buttons Usage - Mobile and Portal view
sections.text:
In mobile view, button titles must clearly indicate that the user is signing in to or signing up for your app using their UAE PASS — not creating a new UAE PASS account through your app.

Mobile View

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
If your interface requires to use Logo-only buttons, make sure

to align logos across multiple sign-in buttons, adjust the button

appearance to coordinate with your UI with guidelines

consideration.
Always make sure that people can instantly identify your button
as a Sign in with UAE PASS button.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
If you choose to display only a “Sign in” or “Login” button, ensure that the UAE PASS button is clearly visible to users upon landing on the login page, following the recommended design guidelines.

Portal Vertical Sign up - Bottom

Portal Sign up vertical option
If you choose to put “Sign up”

button below your native login,
please make sure that UAE PASS
button is clearly appearing to the
users once they land to the login

page.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Portal Sign up vertical option
If you choose to put “Sign up”
button below your native login,
please make sure that UAE PASS
button is clearly appearing to the
users once they land to the login
page.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: If you choose to place the "Sign Up" button above your native login, please ensure that the UAE PASS button remains clearly visible and easily identifiable to users as soon as they land on the login page.

sections.heading: intro
sections.text:
Portal Option A

If your platform provides UAE
PASS digital signature option,
please follow the below guidelines
depending on your use case.

Case A: If UAE PASS is the only
digital signature option that you
provide make sure you use “Sign
with UAE PASS” button as shown
in the example.

Case B: If you use multiple digital
signature options, make sure you
state a clear text that says “Sign
with UAE PASS”. There are two
options in this case: Text only
selection and text with logo.
Please use what best matches
with your platform.

Case B - Text only

Case C - Text with logo

sections.heading: intro
sections.text: To include the UAE PASS slogan, place the text "What is UAE PASS?" directly below the button with a 10px vertical spacing. This text acts as a tooltip trigger to display the slogan: "UAE PASS is a single trusted digital identity for all citizens, residents, and visitors."
